prs.title: refactor: reorganize Streamlit chat UI, add environment variable supp…
prs.body:
This pull request refactors the AI assistant chat and report generation workflow in `app.py` to improve user experience and maintain chat history more robustly. It also enhances the agent prompt in `src/agent.py` to enforce stricter role boundaries and uses a more advanced language model.

**User interface and chat experience improvements:**

* Refactored the chat area in `app.py` to display chat history in a stable container, ensuring that messages persist and are clearly separated from report generation controls. The chat input is now consistently pinned at the bottom, and `st.rerun()` is used to refresh the interface after new messages. 

**Agent prompt and model enhancements:**

* Updated the prompt in `src/agent.py` to strictly define the assistant's role, add guardrails against off-topic queries, and clarify response structure, ensuring the assistant only answers relevant questions and politely declines unrelated ones.
* Upgraded the language model used by the agent from `"llama2-70b-4096"` to `"openai/gpt-oss-120b"` for improved response quality.

**Environment management:**

* Added loading of environment variables from a `.env` file at the top of `app.py` using `dotenv`, improving configuration management.

prs.title: feat: Add PDF report generator (Part 6) + fix CSV feature alignment
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a major new feature: a PDF report generator for the Solar Grid Optimization Assistant, and also improves the robustness of the artifact loading process. The PDF generator creates a structured, downloadable report summarizing forecast data, risk periods, and optimization recommendations. Additionally, the artifact loader now uses feature alignment to better handle varied CSV input formats.

**PDF Report Generation:**

* Added `src/report_gen.py`, which implements a comprehensive PDF report generator using a custom `SolarReportPDF` class based on `fpdf`. The report includes sections for forecast summary, risk periods, and LLM-driven recommendations, with automatic formatting and section splitting based on the input text.

**Robustness Improvements:**

* Updated the artifact loading logic in `app.py` to use a feature aligner (`align_features`) instead of relying on column names matching exactly, making the prediction pipeline more robust to different CSV formats.

prs.title: feat: Add Knowledge Base & RAG system
prs.body:
- Add 4 knowledge documents (grid management, battery storage, energy optimization, solar variability)
- Implement RAG retriever using sentence-transformers + sklearn NearestNeighbors
- Auto-generates FAISS-compatible index for similarity search

fix: add validation support for Union schema types
prs.body:
This PR implements validation for Union schema types to properly validate required fields and custom validators in union schemas and their array elements.

Breaking Changes: None

Test Results: All 3946 tests passing with 5 new Union validation tests

This commit message follows best practices with:

Clear prefix (fix:) indicating the type of change
Concise subject line summarizing the main change
Detailed body explaining what was changed and why
List of specific files and modifications
Reference to the issue being fixed
Test coverage information

prs.title: Revert "Improved Home Page And Contactus Page"
prs.body: Reverts nst-sdc/iris#13

prs.title: Fix(lexer): prevent false positive matching of 'of' keyword in string…
prs.body:
… literals

The eachOf regex was incorrectly matching the word 'of' inside string values when parsing 'each...in' loops, causing syntax errors for valid code.

Example that previously failed:
  each link in ['Terms of service', 'Privacy policy', 'License']
    a= link

Changed the regex from /^(?:each|for) (.*?) of *([^\n]+)/ to require:
- Explicit whitespace around the 'of' keyword
- Valid variable patterns (identifier or [key, value] destructuring)

This ensures 'of' is recognized as a keyword only when used as such, not when it appears within string literals in the iterable expression.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Removed Features**
  * The "Doubts" page and its related navigation link have been removed from the application.
  * All functionality for viewing, posting, and answering academic doubts is no longer available.
  * Several custom UI components and styling configurations were removed, resulting in a simplified interface.
* **Chores**
  * Unused dependencies and configuration files have been deleted to streamline the project.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Amrit
prs.body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * File uploads are now stored and managed using Cloudinary cloud storage, enabling more reliable and scalable file handling.
  * Download tracking is implemented for notes, allowing better monitoring of downloads.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved error handling and messaging for file upload and download operations, including session expiration and missing authentication.
  * Enhanced validation for note uploads, requiring file, title, and subject fields.

* **Refactor**
  * Notes browsing and upload pages have been restyled for a more modern and user-friendly interface.
  * API endpoints and file URLs have been standardized for consistency and compatibility with external storage.
  * Password reset routes now return a "Not Implemented" response.

* **Chores**
  * Updated dependencies to include Cloudinary and related packages for cloud storage support.
  * Removed unused environment variable template and obsolete authentication logic.
  * Cleaned up code comments and unused imports for improved maintainability.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

issues.title: Tagging UI improvements: add tag input with autocomplete

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
Improve the note upload and edit flows by adding a tagging UI with autocomplete. Users should be able to add multiple tags to each note and get suggestions from existing tags or subjects to keep tags consistent.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Add a tag input component with autocomplete suggestions in the upload and edit note forms.
- Suggestions are loaded from the backend or a static list of existing tags/subjects.
- Tags are saved with note metadata and displayed on note cards and detail pages.
- UI is accessible, mobile-friendly, and easy to use.

**Labels:** frontend, enhancement, help wanted

issues.title: Implement pagination / infinite scroll for notes listing

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
Currently, the notes listing page loads all results at once, which can be slow and inefficient for large datasets. Implement pagination or infinite scroll to improve performance and user experience.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- The backend API supports pagination (page/limit) or cursor-based loading for notes queries.
- The frontend notes browsing page is updated to use pagination controls or infinite scrolling to fetch more notes as the user scrolls.
- Existing filters and sorting options continue to work with paginated results.
- Loading indicators and graceful error handling are present during data loads.
- UI is responsive and works on mobile and desktop.

**Labels:** frontend, enhancement, help wanted

issues.title: CONTRIBUTING.md, issue & PR templates, and roadmap

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
Add clear contributor docs and templates to make onboarding contributors easier.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- CONTRIBUTING.md with dev setup, coding conventions, and commit guidelines.
- Issue and PR templates added in .github/.
- Simple public roadmap or project board section.


**Labels:** documentation, good first issue, help wanted, hacktoberfest

issues.title: Bug: Handle file upload errors gracefully
issues.body:
Improve error handling during note file uploads so users see clear messages when uploads fail (e.g., due to size limits, unsupported formats, or network issues). Log errors for debugging.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Show user-friendly error messages on upload failures.
- Log error details for admins.
- Prevent partial/incomplete uploads from being stored.

**Labels:** bug, hacktoberfest

issues.title: Create Difficulty Estimation Utility Function
issues.body:
Create a backend function that analyzes a note's content and estimates its difficulty using AI (OpenAI).

Implement logic using one of the following:

Readability scoring libraries (e.g., textstat)

Keyword frequency/complexity detection

OR use OpenAI API with a custom prompt

Return: "Basic", "Intermediate", or "Advanced"

issues.title: Add Difficulty Field to Note Schema
issues.body: Extend the Mongoose Note model to include a new difficulty field to store the estimated difficulty level (e.g., "Basic", "Intermediate", "Advanced").

issues.title: Fix Auth State Sync and Logout Behavior
issues.body:
There are current issues with how authentication state is managed in the app. Specifically:

After login, protected routes (like Browse and Notes) are not accessible until the page is refreshed.

The Logout button doesn't appear immediately after logging in — it only shows up after refreshing the page.

This indicates that the auth state is not being persisted or synced properly across route/page transitions.

issues.body: Use the pre-configured cloudinary.js module to upload note files (PDF/DOC) to Cloudinary instead of storing in /uploads.

issues.title: Improve UI Design of Home Page
issues.body: The current Home Page is a single-screen layout and looks too basic. This issue aims to redesign and improve the overall user interface (UI) of the Home Page to make it more engaging, modern, and informative.

issues.title: Replace Login/Signup with Logout After Successful Signup/Login

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
After a user signs up or logs in, the navigation bar should update to reflect the authenticated state. The "Login" and "Signup" buttons should be hidden, and a single "Logout" button should be displayed instead.

Acceptance Criteria:

✅ On successful login or signup:

"Login" and "Signup" buttons disappear

"Logout" button appears

✅ On logout:

"Login" and "Signup" buttons reappear

"Logout" button disappears

✅ Token should be cleared from localStorage on logout

issues.title: Registered Users Unable to Login — "Wrong Credentials" Issue
issues.body:
After a new user successfully registers through the signup form, when they try to log in using the same credentials, they receive an error:

"Incorrect email or password" 

This means something is breaking between the registration process and login verification, possibly due to password hashing or database mismatch.

issues.title: Redirect After Signup Should Go to Login Page, Not Browse Notes
issues.body:
Currently, after a user successfully signs up, they are being redirected to the Browse Notes page.
However, the expected user journey should be:

Signup ➝ Login ➝ Home page ➝ Then Browse Notes (if logged in)

issues.title: Restrict Access to Protected Routes (Browse & Upload Notes) Without Login
issues.body:
Currently, users can directly access the Browse Notes and Upload Notes pages without being authenticated.

These pages should be protected so that:

If the user is not logged in, clicking on those buttons should redirect them to the /login page.

If the user is logged in, allow access to these pages.

Fields:

issues.title: Create JWT Auth Middleware
issues.body:
Create an Express middleware function to protect private routes. It should:

Read the JWT token from Authorization header (Bearer <token>)

Verify the token using jsonwebtoken

If valid, attach the user object (userId, etc.) to req.user

If missing or invalid, return 401 Unauthorized

issues.title: Create Protected Routes (PrivateRoute wrapper)
issues.body:
Create a wrapper component (like PrivateRoute) to protect specific routes (e.g., Upload).

Check for valid JWT in localStorage

If not present, redirect to /login

If valid, allow route access

issues.title: Integrate Auth Pages with Backend
issues.body:
Connect both login and registration forms to backend API using Axios.

On success, store JWT token in localStorage.

Redirect to the dashboard or protected page upon login/signup.

Handle errors like wrong credentials or failed signup gracefully.

issues.title: Create Login Route with Credential Validation
issues.body:
Create the backend logic for user login. This should verify credentials, compare the password, and return a JWT on success.

Tasks : 
Find user by email

 Compare hashed password with bcryptjs

 Generate and return JWT token

 Handle invalid credentials

issues.title: Add Register Route with Password Hashing & JWT
issues.body:
Create the route and controller to handle new user registration. Passwords must be hashed using bcryptjs, and duplicate emails should be checked before creation.

Tasks :

        email: String, required, unique
        
        password: String, required

issues.body:
Design and implement the Home Page (Landing Screen) of the Notes Sharing Platform using React. This page will serve as the entry point for users and should clearly showcase the platform’s purpose and core features. The design should be clean, responsive, and easy to navigate.

Tasks :

Include navigation buttons: Sign Up, Login, Browse Notes, Upload Notes

issues.title: Login page creation
issues.body:
Design and implement the Login Page for the Notes Sharing Platform using React. This page will allow existing users to log into their account using their credentials. The page should be clean, responsive, and user-friendly.

Include input fields for:
Email
Password

Add form validation:  Required fields , Valid email format

issues.title: Sign Up page creation
issues.body:
Design and implement the Sign Up Page for the Notes Sharing Platform using React . This page will allow new users to create an account by providing their credentials. It should be clean, responsive, and user-friendly. 

 Include input fields for:
 Full Name
 Email
 Password
 Confirm Password
 Add form validation (e.g., required fields, password match, valid email)
